interpretation: IFIT1B, a protein homologous to IFIT1, is likely non-functional and does not play a significant role in the antiviral immune response. This lack of function suggests that IFIT1B may not be directly involved in the mechanisms underlying Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), which is characterized by bone necrosis due to steroid use. The retrieved evidence indicates that IFIT1B does not inhibit viral RNA translation, which is a critical function of its active counterpart, IFIT1. Consequently, the evidence connecting IFIT1B to SONFH is weak, leading to the inference that it may not serve as a reliable biomarker for early detection of this condition. Overall, the clinical relevance of IFIT1B as a biomarker for SONFH is low, given its non-functional status and lack of direct association with the disease.
relevance_summary: IFIT1B lacks functional relevance to the mechanisms of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), suggesting it is unlikely to be a useful biomarker.

result.title: IFIT1B (Q5T764)
result.text:
Protein: Protein IFIT1 homolog B
Function: IFIT1B is likely non-functional, lacking the critical antiviral role of IFIT1. Unlike IFIT1, which is essential in the innate immune response as part of an interferon-dependent multiprotein complex, IFIT1B does not prevent the translation of viral RNAs that lack host-specific 2'-O-methylation at their 5' cap. Consequently, it probably cannot inhibit their translation by competing with the host translation machinery

result.title: IFIT1B ↔ Pancreatic pseudocyst
result.text:
Disease: Pancreatic pseudocyst
Description: Cyst-like space not lined by epithelium and contained within the pancreas. Pancreatic pseudocysts are often associated with pancreatitis.
Association score: 0.325

result.title: IFIT1B ↔ pathological myopia
result.text:
Disease: pathological myopia
Description: Excessive axial myopia associated with complications (especially posterior staphyloma and CHOROIDAL NEOVASCULARIZATION) that can lead to BLINDNESS.
